issue.title: TOCTOU: call Path::metadata after Path::exists
issue.body: What it does: detect when calling Path::exists followed by Path::metadata, causing a second syscall. Example code is provided. Expected behavior: lint suggests if let Ok(md) = path.metadata(). Suggested fix: implement the lint and add UI tests for the diagnostic.

repository.description: Rust lints for compiler and cargo workflows

reasons: Fresh Rust/compiler issue with clear implementation and validation.

issue.title: Document and test backpressure behavior for bounded mpsc receiver lag
issue.body: The bounded mpsc channel docs do not explain receiver lag behavior under load. Steps to reproduce: spawn producers faster than a receiver and observe send wait time. Expected behavior: docs and tests explain backpressure. Suggested fix: add a small concurrency test and update docs.

repository.description: Asynchronous runtime for Rust backend systems

reasons: Rust backend/concurrency issue with validation path.

issue.title: [DX] dockerize Go app into Dockerfile for testing and production
issue.body: Create Dockerfile.local and production Dockerfile for a Go backend app. Expected behavior: developers can run the API in Docker and CI can build the production image. Suggested fix: add multi-stage Dockerfile and document docker compose validation.

reasons: Strong backend fallback candidate when trusted Rust/Go results are sparse.

issue.title: Try and reduce deduplication in asm-parser-ewavr
issue.body: It is a copy paste of another parser with subtle changes. It would be useful to see whether anything beneficial can be deduplicated. No concrete test path is provided.

repository.description: Compiler Explorer frontend and parser tooling

reasons: Compiler-related but not Rust/Go backend and weakly scoped.

issue.title: [Good First Issue] Add new Japan Fact 237 - Beginner-Friendly Open-source Contribution
issue.body: No prerequisites needed. No code required. Add one new Japan fact from the browser in under 60 seconds.

reasons: No-code education microtask is not backend work.

issue.title: VSCode Extension: Ability to integrate with different LLM service providers
issue.body: A UI to add API keys and enable or disable various AI based features.

comments.body: Could you kindly assign me this issue?

reasons: Generic Python/VSCode LLM service UI issue is not Rust/Go backend systems work and appears claimed.

issue.title: Backend traits for Persistence layer
issue.body: Add backend traits for the persistence layer. More details should be discussed in the implementation.

reasons: Profile relevant but old and underspecified.

issue.title: Observer implementation for seeing the runtime status
issue.body: There should be a UI interface to see what is going on inside the system. That will be a dashboard. Considerations: this can be feature gateable or another crate under a workspace.

reasons: Very old broad dashboard feature request should not become the Rust/Go front page when recall is sparse.
